original: Philip Augustus becomes king of France
Peasant uprising in Transylvania
Battle of Chojnice: The Teutonic army heavily defeats a Polish army led by King Casimir IV during the Thirteen Years' War in northwestern Poland
New Hampshire becomes a county in Massachusetts Bay Colony
Turkey & Austria sign peace treaty - Austria cedes Belgrade to Turks
Fort Ticonderoga in New York opens
Battle of Quebec ends, French surrender to British who capture Quebec City
John Harris of Boston, Massachusetts, builds 1st spinet piano
First loan is raised to pay the salaries of the US President and Congress
Royal Opera House in London opens
Chile declares independence from Spain, celebrated today as Chile's National Day
Anti-Corn Law League established in Great Britain by Richard Cobden
First edition of the Pittsburgh Post-Gazette is published
Baseball rules 1st baseman can tag base for out instead of runner
De Kempenae's Dutch government resigns
First edition of The New York Times is published for 2 cents a copy
Confederate armies officially divide into corps
Battle of Martinsburg, Virginia
Hood's Tennessee campaign begins (also called The Franklin–Nashville Campaign), American Civil War
Government bond agent Jay Cooke & Co collapses, causing panic on Wall Street and the start of the Panic of 1873 and the Long Depression
Pacific Stock Exchange opens (as Local Security Board)
Riots break out in Montreal, Canada to protest against mandatory smallpox vaccinations
The Seneca tribe gives Harriet Maxwell Converse an honorary position as chief, making her the first white woman to become a Native American chief
Daniel David Palmer of Davenport, Iowa, gives the first chiropractic adjustment
Gustave Kecker and Hugh Martin's musical "Belle of New York City" premieres in NYC
Phillies' Chick Fraser no-hits Chicago Cubs, 10-0
Electric tramline opens in Rotterdam
A typhoon with tsunami kills an estimated 10,000 people in Hong Kong
Cleveland Indian Bob "Dusty" Rhoades no-hits Boston, 2-1
Largest paid baseball attendance (35,409), A's beat Tigers, 2-0 in Det
25,000 demonstrate in Amsterdam for general male/female suffrage
Britain's first twin-engine airplane (Short S.39) is test flown
Irish Home Rule Bill receives Royal Assent
South African troops land in German South West Africa
Dutch second chamber accepts female suffrage
Hurricane tides reach 16 feet above normal drowning 280 people along US Gulf Coast
2nd government of Ruys de Beerenbrouck installed in Netherlands
Browns George Sisler's 41-game hit streak is stopped by NY's Joe Bush
Hungary admitted to League of Nations
Government routes 7 Provinces to Peking
Hurricane hits Miami, kills 250
The Columbia Broadcasting System goes on the air. with 18 stations (and WOR as NYC affiliate)
Juan de la Cierva flies 1st helicopter above Channel

San Felipe Segundo/Okeechobee hurricane makes fifth and final landfall near Edisto Island, South Carolina, as category 1 storm; total impact of event - over 4,100 killed and $100M ($1.6B in 2020) in damage
St. Louis beat Phillies, 4-2 at the Baker Bowl, Philadelphia; 20th Cards' win of 22 starts between the 2 clubs in 1928
Pirates loss to Braves & clinch NL pennant for the Cubs
Preston Sturges' "Strictly Dishonorable" premieres in NYC
Enterprise (US) beats Shamrock V (UK) in 15th America's Cup
Philadelphia A's win AL championship for 2nd year in a row
Mukden Incident: To create a pretext for the invasion of Manchuria, China, the Japanese detonate a small quantity of dynamite close to a railway line owned by Japan's South Manchuria Railway
St Louis Brown Bobo Newsom loses no-hitter to Boston in 10, 2-1
USSR admitted to League of Nations
The first New Zealand State house opens in Miramar
Chicago Bears beat Green Bay Packers 2-0
Despite losing a double header (4-3, 8-7) to the St. Louis Browns at Sportsman's Park III, the New York Yankees clinch their 10th AL pennant

19 German aircraft shot down above England
Elmer Harris' "Johnny Belinda" premieres in NYC
Italian troops conquer Sidi Barrani
Canadian Broadcasting Corporation authorized for radio service
The order for "extermination of asocials through labour" is approved by Nazi Minister of Justice, Otto Thierack
Cardinals clinch NL pennant
WWII: British submarine Tradewind torpedoes Junyo Maru: 5,600 killed, including 1,377 allied POWs and 4,200 Javanese slave laborers
WWII: Eindhoven, Netherlands freed by American and British troops (Lightly Day)
WWII: US 266th division defeats German troops at Brest Bretagne in Brittany, France after a 7 week siege; 37,000 prisoners taken, ports rendered useless
1000 whites walk out of Gary, Indiana, schools to protest integration
US Air Force created as a separate military service by passing of the National Security Act
US Department of Defense 1st day of operation
Communist Madiun-uprising in Dutch Indies (Muso/Sjarifudin)
Baseball major league record 4 grand slams hit
Nakagawa Soen, Japanese Zen teacher, receives dharma transmission
"The Day the Earth Stood Still", science fiction film directed by Robert Wise, and starring Michael Rennie, opens
Cleveland Indians clinch AL pennant, beat Tigers (3-2)
KTUL TV channel 8 in Tulsa, OK (ABC) begins broadcasting
WLOS TV channel 13 in G'ville-Spartanburg, SC (ABC) 1st broadcast
Electric train joining in Amsterdam-Brussels
US Western TV drama "Wagon Train" premieres
The Fresno Drop: Bank of America mails out 60,000 BankAmericards in Fresco, California, the first credit card (later renamed VISA)
Vanguard 3 launched into Earth orbit
USSR performs nuclear test at Novaya Zemlya USSR
Bob Aspromonte sets NL 3rd baseman record of 57 cons errorless games
Charlie Finley is denied permission to move As to Dallas-Fort Worth

Final MLB game at Polo Grounds, New York City; 1,752 see Philadelphia Phillies defeat New York Mets 5-1
USSR orders 58.5 million barrels of cereal from Australia

Kiss' first "unmasked" (no make up) appearance on MTV

New Orleans Saints 1st OT victory; beating Chicago Bears 34-31

Tigers clinch AL East championship (spent all year in 1st place)

BBC Radio reports prime # 2^216091-1 found in Houston

Art Modell1st meets (he claims) with Baltimore to move Browns there from Cleveland
Space shuttle STS-69 (Endeavour 9), lands

Internet Corporation for Assigned Names and Numbers (ICANN) is formed.

The United Kingdom's Local Government Act 2003, repealing controversial Section 28 of the Local Government Act 1988, receives Royal Assent
"Watching You" single released by Rodney Atkins (Billboard Song of the Year 2007)

Right-wing protesters riot at the building of Hungarian Television in Budapest, Hungary, one day after an audio tape is made public in which Prime Minister Ferenc Gyurcsány admits he and his party lied during the 2006 general election
Buddhist monks join anti-government protesters in Myanmar, starting what some called the 'Saffron Revolution'
Final episode of soap opera "Guiding Light" is broadcast, ending a 72-year run on radio and television

Transair Sweden DC-6 passenger aircraft chartered by the United Nations for transit to cease-fire negotiations during the Congo Crisis, crashed near Ndola, Northern Rhodesia (present-day Zambia), killing 16, including UN Secretary-GeneralDag Hammarskjöld, the second Secretary-General of the United Nations, and 15 others; official inquiries never determined the cause
